candidate_llm_application: PYSANKA-QUAD — 4-fold rotational-symmetry regularizer + defect-detector for attention Q/K weight blocks. Partition Q/K weights into block-pairs W_h in R^{2d x 2d}; define 90-degree rotation R = [[0,-I],[I,0]] in selected planes; training loss adds penalty L_sym = beta * sum_h sum_{k=1,2,3} ||W_h - R^k W_h R^{-k}||_F^2 to encourage 4-fold rotational symmetry on selected blocks; inference-time eps_h functions as DEFECT DETECTOR — blocks with eps_h > tau flagged as 'cracked' symmetry sites correlating with hallucination/adversarial-vulnerability.
rule: hit iff kw_count>=2 OR sem_score>=0.7 OR func_score>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL

results.title: Platonic Transformers 2510.03511 — attention relative to symmetry-group reference frames; principled weight-sharing for equivariance
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN. Platonic Transformers formalize attention relative to symmetry-group reference frames with principled weight-sharing for group-equivariance — directly subsumes PYSANKA-QUAD's mechanism of enforcing R^k W_h R^{-k} = W_h on Q/K blocks. The cyclic-group C_4 (4-fold rotational) is a discrete Platonic point-group sub-instance covered by the general framework. The 'selected coordinate plane rotation' is the canonical group-action on the embedding space already exposed in Platonic Transformers. Keyword overlap on '4-fold rotational symmetry weight block constraint' / 'selected-coordinate-plane rotation equivariance penalty' concepts.

results.title: Symmetry Breaking Transformers 2601.22257 — Q-K (O(dk)) + V-O (O(dv)) sectors; symmetry-breaking drift
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN. Symmetry Breaking Transformers explicitly factor transformer attention into Q-K (O(dk)) and V-O (O(dv)) symmetry sectors and characterize 'symmetry-breaking drift' — this IS the symmetry-violation defect detector PYSANKA-QUAD proposes (eps_h as Frobenius residual from group-invariance on Q/K weights). The cyclic C_4 sub-group of O(dk) is a strict specialization. The drift-as-diagnostic perspective is identical to PYSANKA-QUAD's eps_h > tau flag. The 4-fold quadrant cultural framing supplies narrative, not mechanism.

results.title: SBDet 2408.11760 — fourth-order cyclic rotation group; relaxed weight constraints + symmetry-breaking detection
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN. SBDet directly addresses C_4 (fourth-order cyclic rotation) symmetry detection with relaxed weight constraints + symmetry-breaking detection — which IS PYSANKA-QUAD's exact group choice (k=1,2,3 with 90-degree rotation R) plus identical defect-detection role. The relaxed-weight-constraint formulation is equivalent to PYSANKA-QUAD's soft penalty L_sym (rather than hard constraint), and SBDet's symmetry-breaking detection is the same eps_h > tau crack-flagging interpretation. The 4-fold pysanka quadrant narrative supplies cosmological motivation only.

results.title: EquiLLM 2502.11149 — E(3)-equivariant LLM with geometry-aware adapter
results.rationale: EquiLLM operationalizes group-equivariance constraints within LLMs via geometry-aware adapter. While EquiLLM uses continuous E(3) instead of discrete C_4, the equivariance-constraint design pattern (force W invariant under group action) is identical to PYSANKA-QUAD's L_sym penalty. Restricting from E(3) to its cyclic C_4 subgroup with 90-degree rotation in coordinate planes is a trivial specialization, not a novel mechanism. Functional-score hit because group-equivariance-via-weight-constraint is the operative principle shared between the two.

results.title: JMLR 2025 Symmetry Unified — nuclear-norm penalties of symmetry-encoding operators
results.rationale: JMLR 2025 Unified Symmetry framework uses nuclear-norm penalties of symmetry-encoding operators to promote group-invariance of learned weights — this IS the symmetry-regularizer paradigm of PYSANKA-QUAD with L_sym = beta * sum ||W_h - R^k W_h R^{-k}||_F^2. The Frobenius-norm penalty PYSANKA-QUAD uses is a sibling-norm to JMLR's nuclear-norm and operationally serves the identical 'promote group-invariance via convex penalty on operator deviation' role. The cyclic C_4 case is in scope.

results.title: Recurrent Equivariant Constraint Modulation 2602.02853 — per-layer learnable symmetry relaxation
results.rationale: Recurrent Equivariant Constraint Modulation introduces per-layer learnable symmetry relaxation — directly anticipates PYSANKA-QUAD's per-block eps_h residual that quantifies how much each block deviates from exact equivariance. The block-level / layer-level modulation of equivariance strength is the same architectural axis as PYSANKA-QUAD's per-head Frobenius residual. PYSANKA-QUAD's defect-detector role (eps_h > tau crack flag) is a thresholded form of Recurrent Equivariant's modulation parameter.

results.title: Constraint Relaxation Equivariant Training 2408.13242 — progressively-constrained non-equivariance term
results.rationale: Constraint Relaxation Equivariant Training adds a progressively-constrained non-equivariance term that converges to equivariant solution — operationally identical to PYSANKA-QUAD's soft Frobenius penalty L_sym = beta * sum ||W_h - R^k W_h R^{-k}||_F^2 with the implicit beta-annealing logic. The 'non-equivariance term' IS eps_h. PYSANKA-QUAD's inference-time eps_h-as-defect-detector use is the natural diagnostic readout of this paper's non-equivariance term.

results.title: Reflection-Rotation Symmetry Detection CVPR 2022 — equivariant-learning-based symmetry detection
results.rationale: CVPR 2022 Reflection-Rotation Symmetry Detection uses equivariant-learning to detect symmetry violations — IS the symmetry-violation defect-detector paradigm of PYSANKA-QUAD (eps_h flagging 'cracked' symmetry sites). Rotation-symmetry detection via equivariant learning is structurally identical to using Frobenius residual to flag broken 4-fold rotation invariance in weight blocks. The 'reflection + rotation' coverage includes C_4 / D_4 sub-cases that PYSANKA-QUAD targets.

reason: All 8 results trigger hit under sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7. Three are exact functional twins: (a) Symmetry Breaking Transformers 2601.22257 (sem 0.92, func 0.92) — explicitly factors transformer attention into Q-K (O(dk)) and V-O (O(dv)) symmetry sectors and measures symmetry-breaking drift as diagnostic, identical to PYSANKA-QUAD's eps_h symmetry-violation defect detector on Q/K weights; (b) Platonic Transformers 2510.03511 (sem 0.90, func 0.90) — attention relative to symmetry-group reference frames with principled weight-sharing for equivariance, subsuming the R^k W_h R^{-k} = W_h constraint as a Platonic point-group instance and the cyclic C_4 sub-case; (c) SBDet 2408.11760 (sem 0.88, func 0.86) — explicit fourth-order cyclic (C_4) rotation group with relaxed weight constraints + symmetry-breaking detection, hitting PYSANKA-QUAD's exact group choice and both roles (regularizer + defect-detector). The remaining results redundantly cover the regularizer/equivariance design space: EquiLLM (E(3) LLM equivariance via geometry adapter), JMLR Unified Symmetry (nuclear-norm symmetry-operator penalty), Recurrent Equivariant Constraint Modulation (per-layer learnable symmetry relaxation = per-block eps_h modulation), Constraint Relaxation Equivariant Training (progressively-relaxed non-equivariance term = PYSANKA-QUAD's L_sym with beta-annealing), Reflection-Rotation Symmetry Detection CVPR 2022 (equivariant-learning symmetry detection covering rotation defect detection). Distinct effect clusters above threshold: (1) symmetry-group reference frames in attention with weight-sharing (Platonic); (2) Q-K/V-O sector symmetry-breaking drift as defect (Symmetry Breaking Transformers); (3) C_4 cyclic rotation + relaxed-constraint + symmetry-breaking detection (SBDet); (4) general group-equivariance via weight-penalty regularizer (EquiLLM, JMLR, Recurrent Equivariant, Constraint Relaxation); (5) equivariance-based symmetry-violation detection (CVPR 2022). PYSANKA-QUAD's two claimed contributions — (a) 4-fold rotational symmetry block + (b) symmetry-violation defect-detector for hallucination correlation — decompose exhaustively across these clusters. Specifically, SBDet alone subsumes both (a) and (b); Symmetry Breaking Transformers separately subsumes (b) on the exact Q-K sector PYSANKA-QUAD targets; Platonic Transformers subsumes (a) on the exact attention-weight substrate. total_hits = 8 >> 1, distinct_effect_clusters = 5 >> 1, candidate FAILS.
recommendation: REJECT candidate. PYSANKA-QUAD is fully anticipated by the conjunction of Symmetry Breaking Transformers 2601.22257 + SBDet 2408.11760 + Platonic Transformers 2510.03511. Specifically: (1) the in-attention C_4 weight-block constraint R^k W_h R^{-k} = W_h is a direct specialization of Platonic Transformers' symmetry-group-reference-frame weight-sharing to the cyclic C_4 subgroup, and is also the explicit construction of SBDet's fourth-order cyclic rotation; (2) the symmetry-violation residual eps_h as defect detector on Q/K blocks is IDENTICAL to Symmetry Breaking Transformers' symmetry-breaking drift on the O(dk) Q-K sector, and to SBDet's symmetry-breaking detection on C_4-relaxed weights; (3) the soft Frobenius penalty L_sym = beta * sum ||W_h - R^k W_h R^{-k}||_F^2 is the sibling penalty to JMLR 2025 Unified Symmetry's nuclear-norm-on-symmetry-operator penalty and is exactly the non-equivariance term Constraint Relaxation Equivariant Training 2408.13242 progressively constrains; (4) the per-block eps_h interpretation is anticipated by Recurrent Equivariant Constraint Modulation 2602.02853's per-layer learnable symmetry relaxation; (5) the eps_h > tau crack-flag for defect detection is anticipated by CVPR 2022 Reflection-Rotation Symmetry Detection via Equivariant Learning. The Hutsul pysanka / kistka / 4-fold quadrant cultural framing provides cosmological narrative for properties (4-fold, partition-then-fill, crack-as-defect) that are already standard in the equivariant-transformer literature. To salvage, the candidate would need a property uniquely supplied by the pysanka source that is NOT anticipated, e.g.: (a) a SEQUENCED partition-then-fill curriculum where W_h is first locked to a 4-fold-invariant subspace via projection, then orthogonal complement filled in a strictly later training stage with cryptographic attestation that the invariant subspace is unmodified after stage 1 (operationalizing the kistka 'wax-first-then-dye' temporal asymmetry as a hard verifiable two-stage protocol with auditable boundary, distinct from soft-penalty annealing); (b) a multi-fold spectrum where DIFFERENT heads enforce DIFFERENT cyclic orders C_2, C_3, C_4, C_6 simultaneously with a joint LCM-divisibility constraint operationalizing pysanka's nested multi-fold geometric symmetries (rosette + cross + star), not just uniform C_4; (c) a hallucination-correlation theorem giving formal information-theoretic equivalence between eps_h > tau and downstream-error-divergence bound, beyond empirical correlation. As stated, PYSANKA-QUAD is in the saturated region documented by Platonic Transformers + Symmetry Breaking Transformers + SBDet.
